title: SEDI Age Attestation
description: A derived, state-endorsed age-threshold attestation for Utah's State-Endorsed Digital Identity (Utah Code 63A-20). Satisfies the statutory 'prove a minimum age without disclosing age or birth date' requirement (63A-20-301(1)(e)) by asserting a boolean over/under a named threshold, so a relying party (e.g. an alcohol vendor, 63A-20-204(2)(a)) never receives the birth date. One schema serves every threshold (18, 21, ...) via the ageThreshold field, mirroring the mDL age_over_NN element. The same-holder binding to the sedi-id root is established at presentation time (a bespoke presentation's I2I edges to both credentials), not by an edge here.
$comment: A v1-style attribute ('acm') ACDC: unlike sedi-id (a v2 Aggregate credential), this carries a single attribute block and is fully version-stamped and SAIDified by this repo's keri 1.2.13 oracle.

u.description: Top-level blinding nonce; present => private variant

i.description: AID of the issuer (the State, via the Department of Government Operations)

ri.description: Registry for the issuer's credential status (revocation)

ageThreshold.description: The age threshold in years being attested (e.g. 18, 21)

ageOver.description: True iff, as of asOf, the holder's age is at or above ageThreshold

asOf.description: Date at which the age comparison was evaluated (a point-in-time attestation; 63A-20-303 does not require continuous monitoring)

r.description: SAID of the governing SEDI governance framework (rules)
